shortAnswer: Direct bilirubin is the form of bilirubin already processed by the liver. An elevated value usually indicates bile congestion or a disease of the liver or bile ducts, unlike indirect bilirubin, which tends to rise with increased cell breakdown.
metaDescription: Direct bilirubin elevated? Meaning, normal ranges, and causes explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does my value indicate bile congestion?
Is an ultrasound of the bile ducts useful?
